title: papaver nudicaule subsp. radicatum
intro: Papaver nudicaule subsp. radicatum, also known as Icelandic poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have made it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The plant is rich in alkaloids, including meconic acid and protopine, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing pain, inflammation, and anxiety. Papaver nudicaule subsp. radicatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like insomnia, muscle spasms, and digestive issues.
